verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes epiregulin, a secreted EGF-family peptide hormone. It is synthesized as a transmembrane precursor that undergoes ectodomain shedding (by ADAM proteases), releasing the mature soluble ligand. The shed form circulates and binds EGFR/ERBB4 on target cells in trans — the recruiter (EGFR) is the surface target, not epiregulin itself. While the pro-form is transiently membrane-anchored, the dominant biology is secretion/shedding; the protein body is not stably displayed on the outer leaflet in the way a surface receptor is. No wash-resistant surface anchoring or stable complex partner is documented. Verdict: secreted_only.
